    17→import FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports
    18→
    19→/-!
    20→# Erdős Problem 147
    21→
    22→*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/147](https://www.erdosproblems.com/147)
    23→
    24→[ErSi84] Erdős, P. and Simonovits, M., _Cube-supersaturated graphs and related problems_,
    25→Progress in graph theory (1984).
    26→
    27→[Ja23] Janzer, O., _Disproof of a conjecture of Erdős and Simonovits on the Turán number_,
    28→(2023).
    29→
    30→[Ja23b] Janzer, O., _Disproof of a conjecture of Erdős and Simonovits on the Turán number
    31→(the case r = 3)_, (2023).
    32→-/
    33→
    34→open SimpleGraph
    35→
    36→namespace Erdos147
    37→
    38→/-- An injective graph homomorphism from $H$ to $F$; witnesses that $F$ contains a
    39→subgraph isomorphic to $H$. -/
    40→def ContainsSubgraph {V U : Type*} (F : SimpleGraph V) (H : SimpleGraph U) : Prop :=
    41→  ∃ f : U → V, Function.Injective f ∧ ∀ u v : U, H.Adj u v → F.Adj (f u) (f v)
    42→
    43→/-- The Turán number $\mathrm{ex}(n; H)$: the maximum number of edges in a simple graph on $n$
    44→vertices that contains no copy of $H$ as a subgraph. -/
    45→noncomputable def turanNumber {U : Type*} (H : SimpleGraph U) (n : ℕ) : ℕ :=
    46→  sSup {m : ℕ | ∃ (V : Type) (fv : Fintype V) (F : SimpleGraph V) (dr : DecidableRel F.Adj),
    47→    haveI := fv; haveI := dr;
    48→    Fintype.card V = n ∧ ¬ContainsSubgraph F H ∧ F.edgeFinset.card = m}
    49→
    50→/--
    51→Erdős Problem 147 [ErSi84]:
    52→If $H$ is a bipartite graph with minimum degree $r$ (i.e., every vertex of $H$ has
    53→degree at least $r$, where $r \geq 2$), is it true that there exists
    54→$\varepsilon = \varepsilon(H) > 0$ such that
    55→$$
    56→\mathrm{ex}(n; H) \gg n^{2 - 1/(r-1) + \varepsilon}?
    57→$$
    58→That is, do there exist constants $C > 0$ and $N_0 \in \mathbb{N}$ such that for all $n \geq N_0$:
    59→$$
    60→\mathrm{ex}(n; H) \geq C \cdot n^{2 - 1/(r-1) + \varepsilon}?
    61→$$
    62→
    63→This conjecture was **disproved**: Janzer [Ja23] disproved it for even $r \geq 4$, and
    64→[Ja23b] disproved it for $r = 3$, constructing for any $\delta > 0$ a $3$-regular bipartite
    65→graph $H$ with $\mathrm{ex}(n; H) \ll n^{4/3 + \delta}$.
    66→-/
    67→@[category research solved, AMS 5]
    68→theorem erdos_147 : answer(False) ↔
    69→    ∀ (r : ℕ), 2 ≤ r →
    70→    ∀ (U : Type*) (H : SimpleGraph U) [Fintype U] [DecidableRel H.Adj],
    71→      Nonempty (H.Coloring (Fin 2)) →
    72→      (∀ v : U, r ≤ H.degree v) →
    73→      ∃ ε : ℝ, 0 < ε ∧
    74→        ∃ C : ℝ, 0 < C ∧
    75→          ∃ N₀ : ℕ, ∀ n : ℕ, N₀ ≤ n →
    76→            C * (n : ℝ) ^ ((2 : ℝ) - 1 / ((r : ℝ) - 1) + ε) ≤ (turanNumber H n : ℝ) := by
    77→  sorry
    78→
    79→end Erdos147
    80→

    16→
    17→import FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports
    18→
    19→/-!
    20→# Erdős Problem 146
    21→
    22→Erdős and Simonovits conjectured that the Turán number of any $r$-degenerate bipartite
    23→graph $H$ satisfies $\text{ex}(n; H) = O(n^{2 - 1/r})$.
    24→
    25→*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/146](https://www.erdosproblems.com/146)
    26→
    27→[ErSi84] Erdős, P. and Simonovits, M., *Cube-supersaturated graphs and related
    28→problems*, Progress in graph theory (Waterloo, Ont., 1982), Academic Press,
    29→Toronto, ON, 1984, 203-218.
    30→
    31→[AKS03] Alon, N., Krivelevich, M., and Sudakov, B., *Turán numbers of bipartite
    32→graphs and related Ramsey-type questions*, Combinatorics, Probability and
    33→Computing 12 (2003), no. 5-6, 477-494.
    34→-/
    35→
    36→open SimpleGraph
    37→
    38→namespace Erdos146
    39→
    40→/-- An injective graph homomorphism from $H$ to $F$; witnesses that $F$ contains a
    41→subgraph isomorphic to $H$. -/
    42→def ContainsSubgraph {V U : Type*} (F : SimpleGraph V) (H : SimpleGraph U) : Prop :=
    43→  ∃ f : U → V, Function.Injective f ∧ ∀ u v : U, H.Adj u v → F.Adj (f u) (f v)
    44→
    45→/-- The Turán number $\text{ex}(n; H)$: the maximum number of edges in a simple graph on $n$
    46→vertices that contains no copy of $H$ as a subgraph. -/
    47→noncomputable def turanNumber {U : Type*} (H : SimpleGraph U) (n : ℕ) : ℕ :=
    48→  sSup {m : ℕ | ∃ (V : Type) (fv : Fintype V) (F : SimpleGraph V) (dr : DecidableRel F.Adj),
    49→    haveI := fv; haveI := dr;
    50→    Fintype.card V = n ∧ ¬ContainsSubgraph F H ∧ F.edgeFinset.card = m}
    51→
    52→/-- A graph $G$ is $r$-degenerate if every non-empty finite set of vertices contains
    53→a vertex with at most $r$ neighbors within that set. Equivalently, every induced
    54→subgraph of $G$ has minimum degree at most $r$. -/
    55→def IsRDegenerateGraph {V : Type*} (G : SimpleGraph V) (r : ℕ) : Prop :=
    56→  ∀ (S : Set V), S.Finite → S.Nonempty →
    57→    ∃ v ∈ S, (G.neighborSet v ∩ S).ncard ≤ r
    58→
    59→/--
    60→Erdős-Simonovits Conjecture (Problem #146) [ErSi84]:
    61→If $H$ is a bipartite graph that is $r$-degenerate (i.e., every induced subgraph of $H$
    62→has minimum degree at most $r$), then
    63→$$\text{ex}(n; H) \ll n^{2 - 1/r}.$$
    64→That is, there exists a constant $C > 0$ such that $\text{ex}(n; H) \leq C \cdot n^{2 - 1/r}$
    65→for all $n \geq 1$.
    66→
    67→This is open even for $r = 2$. Alon, Krivelevich, and Sudakov [AKS03] proved the
    68→weaker bound $\text{ex}(n; H) \ll n^{2 - 1/(4r)}$.
    69→-/
    70→@[category research open, AMS 5]
    71→theorem erdos_146 :
    72→    ∀ (r : ℕ), 1 ≤ r →
    73→    ∀ (U : Type*) (H : SimpleGraph U) [Fintype U] [DecidableRel H.Adj],
    74→      Nonempty (H.Coloring (Fin 2)) →
    75→      IsRDegenerateGraph H r →
    76→      ∃ C : ℝ, 0 < C ∧ ∀ n : ℕ, 1 ≤ n →
    77→        (turanNumber H n : ℝ) ≤ C * (n : ℝ) ^ ((2 : ℝ) - 1 / (r : ℝ)) := by
    78→  sorry
    79→
    80→end Erdos146
    81→

    14→limitations under the License.

    16→
    17→import FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports
    18→
    19→/-!
    20→# Erdős Problem 159
    21→
    22→Is there some constant $c > 0$ such that $R(C_4, K_n) \ll n^{2-c}$?
    23→
    24→*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/159](https://www.erdosproblems.com/159)
    25→
    26→[Er81] Erdős, P., *Problems and results in graph theory*.
    27→
    28→[Er84d] Erdős, P., *On some problems in graph theory, combinatorial analysis and
    29→combinatorial number theory*.
    30→
    31→[EFRS78] Erdős, P., Faudree, R.J., Rousseau, C.C., and Schelp, R.H.
    32→
    33→[Sp77] Spencer, J.
    34→-/
    35→
    36→open SimpleGraph
    37→
    38→namespace Erdos159
    39→
    40→/-- An injective graph homomorphism from $H$ to $G$ witnesses that $G$ contains
    41→    a subgraph isomorphic to $H$. -/
    42→def ContainsSubgraph {V U : Type*} (G : SimpleGraph V) (H : SimpleGraph U) : Prop :=
    43→  ∃ f : U → V, Function.Injective f ∧ ∀ u v : U, H.Adj u v → G.Adj (f u) (f v)
    44→
    45→/-- The 4-cycle $C_4$: vertices $\operatorname{Fin} 4$, with $i$ adjacent to $j$ iff they are
    46→    consecutive modulo 4 (i.e., the edges are $0$–$1$, $1$–$2$, $2$–$3$, $3$–$0$). -/
    47→def C4 : SimpleGraph (Fin 4) where
    48→  Adj i j := (i.val + 1) % 4 = j.val ∨ (j.val + 1) % 4 = i.val
    49→  symm := fun _ _ h => h.elim Or.inr Or.inl
    50→  loopless := by intro i; fin_cases i <;> decide
    51→
    52→/-- The graph Ramsey number $R(C_4, K_n)$: the minimum $N$ such that every simple
    53→    graph $G$ on $N$ vertices either contains a copy of $C_4$ as a subgraph, or the
    54→    complement $G^c$ contains a copy of $K_n$ (i.e., $G$ has an independent set of
    55→    size $n$). -/
    56→noncomputable def ramseyC4Kn (n : ℕ) : ℕ :=
    57→  sInf {N : ℕ | ∀ (G : SimpleGraph (Fin N)),
    58→    ContainsSubgraph G C4 ∨ ContainsSubgraph Gᶜ (⊤ : SimpleGraph (Fin n))}
    59→
    60→/--
    61→Erdős Conjecture (Problem 159) [Er81, Er84d]:
    62→
    63→There exists a constant $c > 0$ such that $R(C_4, K_n) \ll n^{2-c}$, i.e.,
    64→$R(C_4, K_n) = O(n^{2-c})$ as $n \to \infty$.
    65→
    66→The Ramsey number $R(C_4, K_n)$ is the minimum $N$ such that every 2-colouring
    67→of the edges of $K_N$ contains a monochromatic $C_4$ in one colour or a
    68→monochromatic $K_n$ in the other colour.
    69→
    70→The current bounds are:
    71→$$n^{3/2} / (\log n)^{3/2} \ll R(C_4, K_n) \ll n^2 / (\log n)^2,$$
    72→where the upper bound is due to Szemerédi [EFRS78] and the lower bound
    73→to Spencer [Sp77]. Improving the upper bound to $n^{2-c}$ for any fixed
    74→$c > 0$ remains open.
    75→-/
    76→@[category research open, AMS 5]
    77→theorem erdos_159 :
    78→    ∃ c : ℝ, 0 < c ∧
    79→    ∃ C : ℝ, 0 < C ∧
    80→    ∃ N₀ : ℕ, ∀ n : ℕ, N₀ ≤ n →
    81→      (ramseyC4Kn n : ℝ) ≤ C * (n : ℝ) ^ (2 - c) := by
    82→  sorry

    84→end Erdos159

    17→import FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports
    18→
    19→/-!
    20→# Erdős Problem 180
    21→
    22→Asks whether for every finite family of graphs, some member's Turán number is within a
    23→constant factor of the family's Turán number.
    24→
    25→*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/180](https://www.erdosproblems.com/180)
    26→
    27→[ErSi82] Erdős, P. and Simonovits, M., 1982.
    28→-/
    29→
    30→namespace Erdos180
    31→
    32→/-- An injective graph homomorphism from $H$ to $G$: $G$ contains a copy of $H$ as a
    33→subgraph. -/
    34→def ContainsSubgraph {V U : Type*} (G : SimpleGraph V) (H : SimpleGraph U) : Prop :=
    35→  ∃ f : U → V, Function.Injective f ∧ ∀ u v : U, H.Adj u v → G.Adj (f u) (f v)
    36→
    37→/-- The Turán number $\operatorname{ex}(n; H)$: the maximum number of edges in a simple graph
    38→on $n$ vertices that contains no copy of $H$ as a subgraph. -/
    39→noncomputable def turanNumber {U : Type*} (H : SimpleGraph U) (n : ℕ) : ℕ :=
    40→  sSup {m : ℕ | ∃ (V : Type) (fv : Fintype V) (F : SimpleGraph V) (dr : DecidableRel F.Adj),
    41→    haveI := fv; haveI := dr;
    42→    Fintype.card V = n ∧ ¬ContainsSubgraph F H ∧ F.edgeFinset.card = m}
    43→
    44→/-- The Turán number for a family $\operatorname{ex}(n; \mathcal{F})$: the maximum number of
    45→edges in a simple graph on $n$ vertices that contains no copy of any member of the family
    46→$\mathcal{F}$ as a subgraph. The family is given as a function from a finite index type
    47→$\iota$ to finite graphs $\text{SimpleGraph}(\text{Fin}(k_i))$. -/
    48→noncomputable def turanNumberFamily
    49→    {ι : Type*} [Fintype ι]
    50→    {k : ι → ℕ} (H : (i : ι) → SimpleGraph (Fin (k i))) (n : ℕ) : ℕ :=
    51→  sSup {m : ℕ | ∃ (V : Type) (fv : Fintype V) (F : SimpleGraph V) (dr : DecidableRel F.Adj),
    52→    haveI := fv; haveI := dr;
    53→    Fintype.card V = n ∧ (∀ i : ι, ¬ContainsSubgraph F (H i)) ∧ F.edgeFinset.card = m}
    54→
    55→/--
    56→Erdős Problem 180 [ErSi82]:
    57→
    58→Is it true that if $\mathcal{F}$ is a finite set of finite graphs, then there exists some
    59→$G \in \mathcal{F}$ such that
    60→$$\operatorname{ex}(n; G) \leq C \cdot \operatorname{ex}(n; \mathcal{F})$$
    61→for some constant $C$ depending on $\mathcal{F}$ and all $n \geq 1$?
    62→
    63→This has been disproved by a folklore counterexample (Hunter): if
    64→$\mathcal{F} = \{H_1, H_2\}$ where $H_1$ is a star and $H_2$ is a matching (both with
    65→$\geq 2$ edges), then $\operatorname{ex}(n; \mathcal{F}) \ll 1$ but
    66→$\operatorname{ex}(n; H_i) \asymp n$ for $1 \leq i \leq 2$.
    67→-/
    68→@[category research solved, AMS 5]
    69→theorem erdos_180 : answer(False) ↔
    70→    ∀ (ι : Type) [Fintype ι] [Nonempty ι]
    71→      (k : ι → ℕ) (H : (i : ι) → SimpleGraph (Fin (k i))),
    72→    ∃ i : ι,
    73→    ∃ C : ℝ, 0 < C ∧
    74→    ∀ n : ℕ, 1 ≤ n →
    75→      (turanNumber (H i) n : ℝ) ≤ C * (turanNumberFamily H n : ℝ) := by
    76→  sorry
    77→
    78→end Erdos180
    79→

    18→
    19→/-!
    20→# Erdős Problem 136
    21→
    22→*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/136](https://www.erdosproblems.com/136)
    23→
    24→Let $f(n)$ be the smallest number of colours required to colour the edges of $K_n$
    25→such that every $K_4$ contains at least 5 colours. Determine the size of $f(n)$.
    26→
    27→Asked by Erdős and Gyárfás [Er97b], who proved that $\frac{5}{6}(n-1) < f(n) < n$ and that
    28→$f(9) = 8$. Erdős believed the upper bound is closer to the truth. In fact the
    29→lower bound is: Bennett, Cushman, Dudek, and Pralat [BCDP22] proved that
    30→$f(n) \sim \frac{5}{6}n$. Joos and Mubayi [JoMu22] found a shorter proof.
    31→
    32→[Er97b] Erdős, P. and Gyárfás, A., *A variant of the classical Ramsey problem* (1997).
    33→
    34→[BCDP22] Bennett, P., Cushman, R., Dudek, A., and Pralat, P. (2022).
    35→
    36→[JoMu22] Joos, F. and Mubayi, D. (2022).
    37→-/
    38→
    39→open Classical Filter
    40→
    41→namespace Erdos136
    42→
    43→/-- An edge coloring of $K_n$ with colors from $\operatorname{Fin} k$, represented as a function
    44→on ordered pairs of vertices. -/
    45→def EdgeColoring (n k : ℕ) : Type := Fin n → Fin n → Fin k
    46→
    47→/-- The set of distinct colors used on edges within vertex subset $S$
    48→under coloring $\chi$ (using `offDiag` to enumerate all ordered pairs of
    49→distinct vertices in $S$). -/
    50→noncomputable def edgeColors {n k : ℕ} (χ : EdgeColoring n k)
    51→    (S : Finset (Fin n)) : Finset (Fin k) :=
    52→  S.offDiag.image (fun p => χ p.1 p.2)
    53→
    54→/-- A coloring $\chi$ of $K_n$ is $K_4$-five-colored if every 4-element vertex subset
    55→has at least 5 distinct colors on its edges. -/
    56→def IsK4FiveColored {n k : ℕ} (χ : EdgeColoring n k) : Prop :=
    57→  ∀ S : Finset (Fin n), S.card = 4 → 5 ≤ (edgeColors χ S).card
    58→
    59→/-- $f(n)$: the minimum number of colors $k$ for which there exists a
    60→$K_4$-five-colored edge coloring of $K_n$. -/
    61→noncomputable def f (n : ℕ) : ℕ :=
    62→  sInf {k : ℕ | ∃ χ : EdgeColoring n k, IsK4FiveColored χ}
    63→
    64→/--
    65→Erdős Problem 136 – asymptotic result
    66→(Bennett–Cushman–Dudek–Pralat [BCDP22]; shorter proof by Joos–Mubayi [JoMu22]):
    67→$f(n) \sim \frac{5}{6}n$. That is, for every $\varepsilon > 0$ and all sufficiently large $n$,
    68→$|f(n) / n - 5/6| < \varepsilon$.
    69→-/
    70→@[category research solved, AMS 5]
    71→theorem erdos_136 :
    72→    ∀ ε : ℝ, 0 < ε → ∀ᶠ n : ℕ in atTop,
    73→      |(f n : ℝ) / (n : ℝ) - 5 / 6| < ε := by
    74→  sorry
    75→
    76→/--
    77→Erdős–Gyárfás bounds [Er97b]:
    78→For all sufficiently large $n$, $\frac{5}{6}(n-1) < f(n) < n$.
    79→-/
    80→@[category research solved, AMS 5]
    81→theorem erdos_136.variants.bounds :
    82→    ∀ᶠ n : ℕ in atTop,
    83→      (5 : ℝ) / 6 * ((n : ℝ) - 1) < (f n : ℝ) ∧ (f n : ℝ) < (n : ℝ) := by
    84→  sorry
    85→
    86→/--
    87→Special value: $f(9) = 8$, proved by Erdős and Gyárfás [Er97b].
    88→-/
    89→@[category research solved, AMS 5]
    90→theorem erdos_136.variants.f9 : f 9 = 8 := by
    91→  sorry
    92→
    93→end Erdos136
    94→

    16→
    17→import FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports
    18→
    19→/-!
    20→# Erdős Problem 581
    21→
    22→Determine the maximum number of edges in a bipartite subgraph that every
    23→triangle-free graph with $m$ edges must contain.
    24→
    25→*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/581](https://www.erdosproblems.com/581)
    26→
    27→[CEG79] Chung, F. R. K., Erdős, P., and Graham, R. L., 1979.
    28→
    29→[Al96] Alon, N., *Bipartite subgraphs*. Combinatorica 16 (1996), 301–311.
    30→-/
    31→
    32→open SimpleGraph
    33→
    34→namespace Erdos581
    35→
    36→/--
    37→The maximum number of edges in a bipartite spanning subgraph of a graph $G$ on
    38→a finite vertex type. A spanning subgraph $H \leq G$ is bipartite iff it admits a
    39→proper 2-coloring. We use `Set.ncard` to count edges, avoiding the need for
    40→decidability instances.
    41→-/
    42→noncomputable def maxBipartiteEdges {V : Type*} [Fintype V]
    43→    (G : SimpleGraph V) : ℕ :=
    44→  sSup {k : ℕ | ∃ H : SimpleGraph V, H ≤ G ∧
    45→    Nonempty (H.Coloring (Fin 2)) ∧
    46→    H.edgeSet.ncard = k}
    47→
    48→/--
    49→$f(m)$ is the largest $k$ such that every triangle-free graph on $m$ edges must
    50→contain a bipartite subgraph with at least $k$ edges. Equivalently, $f(m)$ is
    51→the infimum of `maxBipartiteEdges G` over all finite triangle-free graphs $G$
    52→with exactly $m$ edges. We quantify over graphs on `Fin n` for all $n$, which
    53→suffices since every finite graph is isomorphic to one on `Fin n`.
    54→-/
    55→noncomputable def f (m : ℕ) : ℕ :=
    56→  sInf {k : ℕ | ∃ (n : ℕ) (G : SimpleGraph (Fin n)),
    57→    G.CliqueFree 3 ∧
    58→    G.edgeSet.ncard = m ∧
    59→    maxBipartiteEdges G = k}
    60→
    61→/--
    62→Erdős Problem 581 [CEG79]:
    63→
    64→Let $f(m)$ be the maximal $k$ such that a triangle-free graph on $m$ edges must
    65→contain a bipartite graph with $k$ edges. Determine $f(m)$.
    66→
    67→Resolved by Alon [Al96], who showed that there exist constants $c_1, c_2 > 0$
    68→such that $m/2 + c_1 \cdot m^{4/5} \leq f(m) \leq m/2 + c_2 \cdot m^{4/5}$.
    69→-/
    70→@[category research solved, AMS 5]
    71→theorem erdos_581 :
    72→    ∃ c₁ c₂ : ℝ, 0 < c₁ ∧ 0 < c₂ ∧
    73→      ∀ m : ℕ, 0 < m →
    74→        (m : ℝ) / 2 + c₁ * (m : ℝ) ^ ((4 : ℝ) / 5) ≤ (f m : ℝ) ∧
    75→        (f m : ℝ) ≤ (m : ℝ) / 2 + c₂ * (m : ℝ) ^ ((4 : ℝ) / 5) := by
    76→  sorry
    77→
    78→end Erdos581
    79→

    16→
    17→import FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports
    18→
    19→/-!
    20→# Erdős Problem 740
    21→
    22→*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/740](https://www.erdosproblems.com/740)
    23→
    24→A question of Erdős and Hajnal [Er69b][Er71, p.100][Er81][Er95d].
    25→
    26→Let $\mathfrak{m}$ be an infinite cardinal and $G$ be a graph with chromatic number
    27→$\mathfrak{m}$. Let $r \geq 1$. Must $G$ contain a subgraph of chromatic number
    28→$\mathfrak{m}$ which does not contain any odd cycle of length $\leq r$?
    29→
    30→Rödl proved this is true if $\mathfrak{m} = \aleph_0$ and $r = 3$ (see [108] for
    31→the finitary version).
    32→
    33→More generally, Erdős and Hajnal asked whether for every cardinal $\mathfrak{m}$ and
    34→integer $r$, there exists $f_r(\mathfrak{m})$ such that every graph with chromatic
    35→number $\geq f_r(\mathfrak{m})$ contains a subgraph with chromatic number
    36→$\mathfrak{m}$ with no odd cycle of length $\leq r$.
    37→-/
    38→
    39→open SimpleGraph Cardinal
    40→
    41→universe u
    42→
    43→namespace Erdos740
    44→
    45→/-- The cardinal chromatic number of a graph: the infimum of cardinals $\kappa$
    46→for which $G$ admits a proper $\kappa$-coloring. -/
    47→noncomputable def cardChromaticNumber {V : Type u}
    48→    (G : SimpleGraph V) : Cardinal.{u} :=
    49→  sInf {κ : Cardinal.{u} | ∃ (α : Type u), #α = κ ∧ Nonempty (G.Coloring α)}
    50→
    51→/--
    52→Erdős Problem 740 [Er69b][Er71][Er81][Er95d]:
    53→
    54→If $G$ is a graph with infinite chromatic number $\mathfrak{m}$, then for every
    55→$r \geq 1$, $G$ contains a subgraph with chromatic number $\mathfrak{m}$ that has
    56→no odd cycle of length $\leq r$.
    57→
    58→A question of Erdős and Hajnal. Rödl proved the case
    59→$\mathfrak{m} = \aleph_0$, $r = 3$.
    60→-/
    61→@[category research open, AMS 5]
    62→theorem erdos_740 : answer(sorry) ↔
    63→    ∀ {V : Type u} (G : SimpleGraph V) (𝔪 : Cardinal.{u}),
    64→      ℵ₀ ≤ 𝔪 → cardChromaticNumber G = 𝔪 → ∀ (r : ℕ), 1 ≤ r →
    65→        ∃ (W : Type u) (H : SimpleGraph W),
    66→          cardChromaticNumber H = 𝔪 ∧
    67→          (∃ f : W → V, Function.Injective f ∧ ∀ a b, H.Adj a b → G.Adj (f a) (f b)) ∧
    68→          (∀ (w : W) (p : H.Walk w w), p.IsCycle → Odd p.length → r < p.length) := by
    69→  sorry
    70→
    71→end Erdos740
    72→

    16→import FormalConjecturesForMathlib.Combinatorics.SimpleGraph.Clique
    17→import Mathlib.Data.NNRat.Floor
    18→import Mathlib.Combinatorics.Enumerative.DoubleCounting
    19→import Mathlib.Combinatorics.SimpleGraph.Coloring
    20→import Mathlib.Data.Set.Card
    21→
    22→variable {V α ι : Type*} {G : SimpleGraph V} {n : ℕ}
    23→
    24→namespace SimpleGraph
    25→
    26→@[inherit_doc] scoped notation "χ(" G ")" => chromaticNumber G
    27→
    28→lemma le_chromaticNumber_iff_colorable : n ≤ G.chromaticNumber ↔ ∀ m, G.Colorable m → n ≤ m := by
    29→  simp [chromaticNumber]
    30→
    31→lemma le_chromaticNumber_iff_coloring :
    32→    n ≤ G.chromaticNumber ↔ ∀ m, G.Coloring (Fin m) → n ≤ m := by
    33→  simp [le_chromaticNumber_iff_colorable, Colorable]
    34→
    35→lemma lt_chromaticNumber_iff_not_colorable : n < G.chromaticNumber ↔ ¬ G.Colorable n := by
    36→  rw [← chromaticNumber_le_iff_colorable, not_le]
    37→
    38→lemma le_chromaticNumber_iff_not_colorable (hn : n ≠ 0) :
    39→    n ≤ G.chromaticNumber ↔ ¬ G.Colorable (n - 1) := by
    40→  let n + 1 := n; simp [ENat.add_one_le_iff, lt_chromaticNumber_iff_not_colorable]
    41→
    42→lemma Coloring.injective_comp_of_pairwise_adj (C : G.Coloring α) (f : ι → V)
    43→    (hf : Pairwise fun i j ↦ G.Adj (f i) (f j)) : (C ∘ f).Injective :=
    44→  Function.injective_iff_pairwise_ne.2 fun _i _j hij ↦ C.valid <| hf hij
    45→
    46→lemma Colorable.card_le_of_pairwise_adj (hG : G.Colorable n) (f : ι → V)
    47→    (hf : Pairwise fun i j ↦ G.Adj (f i) (f j)) : Nat.card ι ≤ n := by
    48→  obtain ⟨C⟩ := hG
    49→  simpa using Nat.card_le_card_of_injective _ (C.injective_comp_of_pairwise_adj f hf)
    50→
    51→lemma le_chromaticNumber_of_pairwise_adj (hn : n ≤ Nat.card ι) (f : ι → V)
    52→    (hf : Pairwise fun i j ↦ G.Adj (f i) (f j)) : n ≤ G.chromaticNumber :=
    53→  le_chromaticNumber_iff_colorable.2 fun _m hm ↦ hn.trans <| hm.card_le_of_pairwise_adj f hf
    54→
    55→lemma card_div_indepNum_le_chromaticNumber : ⌈(Nat.card V / α(G) : ℚ≥0)⌉₊ ≤ G.chromaticNumber := by
    56→  cases finite_or_infinite V
    57→  swap; · simp
    58→  cases nonempty_fintype V
    59→  simp only [Nat.card_eq_fintype_card, le_chromaticNumber_iff_coloring, Nat.ceil_le]
    60→  refine fun m c ↦ div_le_of_le_mul₀ (by simp) (by simp) ?_
    61→  norm_cast
    62→  rw [← mul_one (Fintype.card V), ← Fintype.card_fin m]
    63→  refine Finset.card_mul_le_card_mul (c · = ·)
    64→    (by simp [Finset.bipartiteAbove, Finset.filter_nonempty_iff])
    65→    fun b _ ↦ IsIndepSet.card_le_indepNum ?_
    66→  simpa [IsIndepSet, Set.Pairwise] using fun x hx y hy _ ↦ c.not_adj_of_mem_colorClass hx hy
    67→
    68→instance (f : ι → V) : Std.Symm fun i j ↦ G.Adj (f i) (f j) where symm _ _ := .symm
    69→
    70→variable (G) in
    71→/-- A set of edges is critical if deleting them reduces the chromatic number. -/
    72→def IsCriticalEdges (edges : Set (Sym2 V)) : Prop :=
    73→  (G.deleteEdges edges).chromaticNumber < G.chromaticNumber
    74→
    75→variable (G) in
    76→/-- An edge is critical if deleting it reduces the chromatic number. -/
    77→def IsCriticalEdge (e : Sym2 V) : Prop := G.IsCriticalEdges ({e} : Set (Sym2 V))
    78→
    79→/--
    80→A set of vertices is critical if deleting them reduces the chromatic number.
    81→-/
    82→def Subgraph.IsCriticalVerts (verts : Set V) (G' : G.Subgraph) : Prop :=
    83→  (G'.deleteVerts verts).coe.chromaticNumber < G'.coe.chromaticNumber
    84→
    85→/--
    86→A vertex is critical if deleting it reduces the chromatic number.
    87→-/
    88→def Subgraph.IsCriticalVertex (v : V) (G' : G.Subgraph) : Prop := G'.IsCriticalVerts {v}
    89→
    90→variable (G)
    91→
    92→/--
    93→A graph `G` is `k`-critical (or vertex-critical) if its chromatic number is `k`,
    94→and deleting any single vertex reduces the chromatic number.
    95→-/
    96→def IsCritical (k : ℕ) : Prop := G.chromaticNumber = k ∧ ∀ v, (⊤ : G.Subgraph).IsCriticalVertex v
    97→
    98→theorem not_isCritical_of_fintype_lt [Fintype V] (k : ℕ) (hk : Fintype.card V < k) :
    99→   ¬G.IsCritical k := by
   100→  simp [IsCritical]
   101→  intro h
   102→  have := h ▸ SimpleGraph.chromaticNumber_le_iff_colorable.2 G.colorable_of_fintype
   103→  simp at this
   104→  grind
   105→
   106→open SimpleGraph
   107→
   108→theorem colorable_iff_induce_eq_bot (G : SimpleGraph V) (n : ℕ) :
   109→    G.Colorable n ↔ ∃ coloring : V → Fin n, ∀ i, G.induce {v | coloring v = i} = ⊥ := by
   110→  refine ⟨fun ⟨a, h⟩ ↦ ⟨a, by aesop⟩, fun ⟨w, h⟩ ↦ ⟨w, @fun a b h_adj ↦ ?_⟩⟩
   111→  specialize h (w a)
   112→  contrapose h
   113→  intro hG
   114→  have : ¬ ((SimpleGraph.induce {v | w v = w a} G).Adj ⟨a, by rfl⟩ ⟨b, by simp_all⟩) :=
   115→    hG ▸ fun a ↦ a
   116→  exact this h_adj
   117→
   118→def Cocolorable (G : SimpleGraph V) (n : ℕ) : Prop := ∃ coloring : V → Fin n,
   119→  ∀ i, G.induce {v | coloring v = i} = ⊥ ∨ G.induce {v | coloring v = i} = ⊤
   120→
   121→example (G : SimpleGraph V) (n : ℕ) : G.Colorable n → SimpleGraph.Cocolorable G n := by
   122→  simp [colorable_iff_induce_eq_bot, Cocolorable]
   123→  aesop
   124→
   125→/--
   126→The chromatic number of a graph is the minimal number of colors needed to color it.
   127→This is `⊤` (infinity) iff `G` isn't colorable with finitely many colors.
   128→
   129→If `G` is colorable, then `ENat.toNat G.chromaticNumber` is the `ℕ`-valued chromatic number.
   130→-/
   131→noncomputable def cochromaticNumber (G : SimpleGraph V) : ℕ∞ := ⨅ n ∈ setOf G.Cocolorable, (n : ℕ∞)
   132→
   133→/-- The chromatic cardinal is the minimal number of colors need to color it. In contrast to
   134→`chromaticNumber`, which assigns `⊤ : ℕ∞` to all non-finitely colorable graphs, this definition
   135→returns a `Cardinal` and can therefore distinguish between different infinite chromatic numbers. -/
   136→noncomputable def chromaticCardinal.{u} {V : Type u} (G : SimpleGraph V) : Cardinal :=
   137→  sInf {κ : Cardinal | ∃ (C : Type u) (_ : Cardinal.mk C = κ), Nonempty (G.Coloring C)}
   138→
   139→/-- The maximum size of the union of k finite independent sets. -/
   140→noncomputable def indepNumK (G : SimpleGraph V) (k : ℕ) : ℕ :=
   141→  sSup {n | ∃ f : Fin k → Set V, (∀ i, G.IsIndepSet (f i)) ∧ (⋃ i, f i).ncard = n}
   142→
   143→/-- A finite graph is CDS-colorable if it has a proper coloring
   144→by natural numbers such that for all `k > 0`, the number of
   145→vertices with color `< k` equals the maximum size of
   146→the union of `k` independent sets. -/
   147→def CDSColorable [Fintype α] {G : SimpleGraph α} : Prop :=
   148→    ∃ (C : G.Coloring Nat), ∀ k : Nat,
   149→   ∑ i < k, (C.colorClass i).ncard = indepNumK G k
   150→
   151→/-- A homomorphism is rainbow if it maps distinct edges to distinct colors. -/
   152→def IsRainbow {α V : Type*} {H : SimpleGraph α} {G : SimpleGraph V} (f : H →g G) {C : Type*}
   153→    (c : Sym2 V → C) : Prop :=
   154→  Function.Injective fun e : H.edgeSet => c (Sym2.map f e)
   155→
   156→/--
   157→The anti-Ramsey number $\mathrm{AR}(n, H)$: maximum colors to edge-color $K_n$ without rainbow $H$.
   158→-/
   159→noncomputable def antiRamseyNum {α : Type*} [Fintype α] (H : SimpleGraph α) (n : ℕ) : ℕ :=
   160→  sSup {k | ∃ c : Sym2 (Fin n) → Fin k, ∀ f : H →g ⊤, ¬IsRainbow f c}
   161→

    16→
    17→import Mathlib.Combinatorics.SimpleGraph.Acyclic
    18→import Mathlib.Combinatorics.SimpleGraph.Bipartite
    19→import Mathlib.Combinatorics.SimpleGraph.Matching
    20→import Mathlib.Data.Real.Archimedean
    21→import Mathlib.Analysis.InnerProductSpace.PiL2
    22→
    23→namespace SimpleGraph
    24→
    25→open Classical Finset List
    26→
    27→variable {α : Type*} [Fintype α] [DecidableEq α]
    28→
    29→/-- `Ls G` is the maximum number of leaves over all spanning trees of `G`.
    30→It is defined to be `0` when `G` is not connected. -/
    31→noncomputable def Ls (G : SimpleGraph α) [DecidableRel G.Adj] : ℝ :=
    32→  letI spanningTrees := { T : Subgraph G | T.IsSpanning ∧ IsTree T.coe }
    33→  letI leaves (T : Subgraph G) := T.verts.toFinset.filter (fun v => T.degree v = 1)
    34→  letI num_leaves (T : Subgraph G) := (leaves T).card
    35→  sSup (Set.image (fun T => (num_leaves T : ℝ)) spanningTrees)
    36→
    37→/-- `n G` is the number of vertices of `G` as a real number. -/
    38→noncomputable def n (_ : SimpleGraph α) : ℝ := Fintype.card α
    39→
    40→/-- `m G` is the size of a maximum matching of `G`. -/
    41→noncomputable def m (G : SimpleGraph α) [DecidableRel G.Adj] : ℝ :=
    42→  let matchings := { M : Subgraph G | M.IsMatching }
    43→  sSup (Set.image (fun M => (M.edgeSet.toFinset.card : ℝ)) matchings)
    44→
    45→/-- The maximum cardinality among all independent sets `s`
    46→    that maximize the quantity `|s| - |N(s)|`, where `N(s)`
    47→    is the neighborhood of the set `s`. -/
    48→noncomputable def aprime (G : SimpleGraph α) [DecidableRel G.Adj] : ℝ :=
    49→  letI indep_sets : Finset (Finset α) := univ.powerset.filter (fun s => G.IsIndepSet (s : Set α))
    50→  letI diff (s : Finset α) : ℤ := (s.card : ℤ) - (⋃ v ∈ (s : Set α), G.neighborSet v).toFinset.card
    51→  letI max_diff := (indep_sets.image diff).max
    52→  letI critical_sets := indep_sets.filter (fun s ↦ diff s = max_diff.getD 0)
    53→  letI max_card := (critical_sets.image Finset.card).max
    54→  (max_card.getD 0 : ℝ)
    55→
    56→/-- `largestInducedForestSize G` is the size of a largest induced forest of `G`. -/
    57→noncomputable def largestInducedForestSize (G : SimpleGraph α) : ℕ :=
    58→  sSup { n | ∃ s : Finset α, (G.induce s).IsAcyclic ∧ s.card = n }
    59→
    60→/-- The degree sequence of a graph, sorted in nondecreasing order. -/
    61→noncomputable def degreeSequence (G : SimpleGraph α) [DecidableRel G.Adj] : List ℕ :=
    62→  (Finset.univ.val.map fun v : α => G.degree v).sort (· ≤ ·)
    63→
    64→/--
    65→The maximum number of occurrences of any term of the degree sequence of `G`.
    66→-/
    67→noncomputable def degreeSequenceMultiplicity (G : SimpleGraph α) [DecidableRel G.Adj] : ℕ :=
    68→  letI degs := degreeSequence G
    69→  (List.max? (degs.map (fun d => degs.count d))).getD 0
    70→
    71→/-- `largestInducedBipartiteSubgraphSize G` is the size of a largest induced
    72→bipartite subgraph of `G`. -/
    73→noncomputable def largestInducedBipartiteSubgraphSize (G : SimpleGraph α) : ℕ :=
    74→  sSup { n | ∃ s : Finset α, (G.induce s).IsBipartite ∧ s.card = n }
    75→
    76→/-- `b G` is the number of vertices of a largest induced bipartite subgraph of `G`.
    77→Returned as a real number. -/
    78→noncomputable def b (G : SimpleGraph α) : ℝ :=
    79→  (largestInducedBipartiteSubgraphSize G : ℝ)
    80→
    81→/-- Independence number of the neighbourhood of `v`. -/
    82→noncomputable def indepNeighborsCard (G : SimpleGraph α) (v : α) : ℕ :=
    83→  (G.induce (G.neighborSet v)).indepNum
    84→
    85→/-- The same quantity as a real number. -/
    86→noncomputable def indepNeighbors (G : SimpleGraph α) (v : α) : ℝ :=
    87→  (indepNeighborsCard G v : ℝ)
    88→
    89→/-- Average of `indepNeighbors` over all vertices. -/
    90→noncomputable def averageIndepNeighbors (G : SimpleGraph α) : ℝ :=
    91→  (∑ v ∈ Finset.univ, indepNeighbors G v) / (Fintype.card α : ℝ)
    92→
    93→/-- A unit distance graph in ℝ²:
    94→A graph where the vertices V are a collection of points in ℝ² and there is
    95→an edge between two points if and only if the distance between them is 1. -/
    96→def UnitDistancePlaneGraph (V : Set (EuclideanSpace ℝ (Fin 2))) : SimpleGraph V where
    97→  Adj x y := Dist.dist x y = 1
    98→  symm x y := by simp [PseudoMetricSpace.dist_comm]
    99→
   100→/--
   101→Two walks are internally disjoint if they share no vertices other than their endpoints.
   102→-/
   103→def InternallyDisjoint {V : Type*} {G : SimpleGraph V} {u v x y : V}
   104→    (p : G.Walk u v) (q : G.Walk x y) : Prop :=
   105→  Disjoint p.support.tail.dropLast q.support.tail.dropLast
   106→
   107→/--
   108→We say a graph is infinitely connected if any two vertices are connected by infinitely many
   109→pairwise disjoint paths.
   110→-/
   111→def InfinitelyConnected {V : Type*} (G : SimpleGraph V) : Prop :=
   112→  Pairwise fun u v ↦ ∃ P : Set (G.Walk u v),
   113→    P.Infinite ∧ (∀ p ∈ P, p.IsPath) ∧ P.Pairwise InternallyDisjoint
   114→
   115→/-- Infinite graphs: definitions for max degree and clique number so that the maximum
   116→degree (resp. clique number) of a graph with unbounded degree (resp. clique size) is
   117→`∞` rather than 0.
   118→-/
   119→noncomputable
   120→def edegree {V : Type*} (G : SimpleGraph V) (v : V) : ℕ∞ := (G.neighborSet v).encard
   121→
   122→noncomputable
   123→def emaxDegree {V : Type*} (G : SimpleGraph V) : ℕ∞ := ⨆ v, G.edegree v
   124→
   125→noncomputable
   126→def ecliqueNum {V : Type} (G : SimpleGraph V) : ℕ∞ := ⨆ (s : Finset V) (_ : G.IsClique s), #s
   127→
   128→end SimpleGraph
   129→

    16→
    17→import Mathlib.Combinatorics.SimpleGraph.Clique
    18→import Mathlib.SetTheory.Ordinal.Exponential
    19→
    20→open Cardinal Ordinal
    21→
    22→universe u
    23→
    24→/--
    25→This proposition asserts the ordinal Ramsey property `α → (β, c)²`.
    26→
    27→It states that for any 2-coloring of the complete graph on the ordinal `α`,
    28→one of the following must hold:
    29→* There is a red clique which is order-isomorphic to `β`.
    30→* There is a blue clique of cardinality `c`.
    31→-/
    32→def OrdinalCardinalRamsey (α β : Ordinal.{u}) (c : Cardinal.{u}) : Prop :=
    33→  -- For any 2-coloring of `α`,
    34→  ∀ red blue : SimpleGraph α.ToType, IsCompl red blue →
    35→    -- either there is a red `K_β`
    36→    (∃ s, red.IsClique s ∧ typeLT s = β) ∨
    37→   -- or there is a blue `K_c`.
    38→    ∃ s, blue.IsClique s ∧ #s = c
    39→

    16→import FormalConjecturesForMathlib.Combinatorics.SimpleGraph.GraphConjectures.Definitions
    17→import FormalConjecturesForMathlib.Combinatorics.SimpleGraph.GraphConjectures.Domination
    18→import Mathlib.Analysis.Matrix.Spectrum
    19→import Mathlib.Combinatorics.SimpleGraph.AdjMatrix
    20→import Mathlib.Combinatorics.SimpleGraph.Metric
    21→import Mathlib.Data.Multiset.Interval
    22→
    23→noncomputable def Matrix.IsHermitian.maxEigenvalue {𝕜 : Type*} [Field 𝕜] [RCLike 𝕜]
    24→    {n : Type*} [Fintype n] [DecidableEq n] {A : Matrix n n 𝕜} (hA : A.IsHermitian) : ℝ :=
    25→  iSup hA.eigenvalues
    26→
    27→namespace SimpleGraph
    28→
    29→open Classical
    30→
    31→variable {α : Type*} [Fintype α] [DecidableEq α]
    32→
    33→/-- Abbreviation for the average independence number of the neighborhoods.
    34→-/
    35→noncomputable abbrev l (G : SimpleGraph α) : ℝ := averageIndepNeighbors G
    36→
    37→/-- The same quantity under a different name, used in some conjectures.
    38→-/
    39→noncomputable abbrev l_avg (G : SimpleGraph α) : ℝ := averageIndepNeighbors G
    40→
    41→/-- Independent domination number of `G`. -/
    42→noncomputable def gi (G : SimpleGraph α) : ℕ := G.indepDominationNumber
    43→
    44→/-- `temp_v G v = deg(v)/(n(G) - deg(v))`. -/
    45→noncomputable def temp_v (G : SimpleGraph α) [DecidableRel G.Adj] (v : α) : ℝ :=
    46→  let n := Fintype.card α
    47→  let deg := G.degree v
    48→  if n = deg then 0 else (deg : ℝ) / ((n : ℝ) - (deg : ℝ))
    49→
    50→/-- Maximum of `temp_v` over all vertices. -/
    51→noncomputable def MaxTemp (G : SimpleGraph α) [DecidableRel G.Adj] [Fintype α] [Nonempty α] : ℝ :=
    52→  let temps := Finset.univ.image (temp_v G)
    53→  temps.max' (Finset.image_nonempty.mpr Finset.univ_nonempty)
    54→
    55→/-- Cardinality of the union of the neighbourhoods of the ends of the non-edge `e`. -/
    56→def non_edge_neighborhood_card (G : SimpleGraph α) [DecidableRel G.Adj] (e : Sym2 α) : ℕ :=
    57→  Sym2.lift ⟨fun u v => (G.neighborFinset u ∪ G.neighborFinset v).card,
    58→    fun u v => by simp [Finset.union_comm]⟩ e
    59→
    60→/-- Minimum size of the neighbourhood of a non-edge of `G`. -/
    61→noncomputable def NG (G : SimpleGraph α) [DecidableRel G.Adj] : ℝ :=
    62→  let non_edges := (compl G).edgeFinset
    63→  if h : non_edges.Nonempty then
    64→    let neighbor_sizes := non_edges.image (non_edge_neighborhood_card G)
    65→    (neighbor_sizes.min' (Finset.Nonempty.image h _))
    66→  else
    67→    (Fintype.card α : ℝ)
    68→
    69→/-- Size of a largest induced forest of `G` expressed as a real number. -/
    70→noncomputable def S (G : SimpleGraph α) : ℝ :=
    71→  let card := Fintype.card α
    72→  if card < 2 then 0 else
    73→    let degrees := Multiset.ofList (List.map (fun v => G.degree v) Finset.univ.toList)
    74→    let sorted_degrees := degrees.sort (· ≤ ·)
    75→    ↑((sorted_degrees[card - 2]?).getD 0)
    76→
    77→/-- Local eccentricity of a vertex. -/
    78→noncomputable def local_eccentricity (G : SimpleGraph α) (v : α) : ENat :=
    79→  sSup (Set.range (G.edist v))
    80→
    81→/-- The largest integer less than or equal to `x`. -/
    82→noncomputable def FLOOR (x : ℝ) : ℝ := ⌊x⌋
    83→
    84→/-- Eccentricity of a vertex. -/
    85→noncomputable def eccentricity (G : SimpleGraph α) (v : α) : ℕ∞ :=
    86→  sSup (Set.range (G.edist v))
    87→
    88→/-- The minimum eccentricity among all vertices. -/
    89→noncomputable def minEccentricity (G : SimpleGraph α) : ℕ∞ :=
    90→  sInf (Set.range (eccentricity G))
    91→
    92→/-- The set of vertices of minimum eccentricity. -/
    93→noncomputable def graphCenter (G : SimpleGraph α) : Set α :=
    94→  {v : α | eccentricity G v = minEccentricity G}
    95→
    96→/-- The maximum eccentricity among all vertices. -/
    97→noncomputable def maxEccentricity (G : SimpleGraph α) : ℕ∞ :=
    98→  sSup (Set.range (eccentricity G))
    99→
   100→/-- The set of vertices of maximum eccentricity. -/
   101→noncomputable def maxEccentricityVertices (G : SimpleGraph α) : Set α :=
   102→  {v : α | eccentricity G v = maxEccentricity G}
   103→
   104→/-- Distance from a vertex to a finite set. -/
   105→noncomputable def distToSet (G : SimpleGraph α) (v : α) (S : Set α) : ℕ :=
   106→  if h : S.toFinset.Nonempty then
   107→    (S.toFinset.image (fun s => G.dist v s)).min' (Finset.Nonempty.image h _)
   108→  else 0
   109→
   110→/-- Average distance of `G`. -/
   111→noncomputable def averageDistance (G : SimpleGraph α) : ℝ :=
   112→  if Fintype.card α > 1 then
   113→    (∑ u ∈ Finset.univ, ∑ v ∈ Finset.univ, (G.dist u v : ℝ)) /
   114→      ((Fintype.card α : ℝ) * ((Fintype.card α : ℝ) - 1))
   115→  else
   116→    0
   117→
   118→/-- The floor of the average distance of `G`. -/
   119→noncomputable def path (G : SimpleGraph α) : ℕ :=
   120→  if G.Connected then
   121→    Nat.floor (averageDistance G)
   122→  else
   123→    0
   124→
   125→/-- Auxiliary quantity `ecc` used in conjecture 34. -/
   126→noncomputable def ecc (G : SimpleGraph α) (S : Set α) : ℕ :=
   127→  let s_comp := Finset.univ.filter (fun v => v ∉ S)
   128→  if h : s_comp.Nonempty then
   129→    (s_comp.image (fun v => distToSet G v S)).max' (Finset.Nonempty.image h _)
   130→  else 0
   131→
   132→/-- Average distance from all vertices to a given set. -/
   133→noncomputable def distavg (G : SimpleGraph α) (S : Set α) : ℝ :=
   134→  if Fintype.card α > 0 then
   135→    (∑ v ∈ Finset.univ, (distToSet G v S : ℝ)) / (Fintype.card α : ℝ)
   136→  else
   137→    0
   138→
   139→/-- A family of paths covering all vertices without overlaps. -/
   140→def IsPathCover (G : SimpleGraph α) (P : Finset (Finset α)) : Prop :=
   141→  (∀ s1 ∈ P, ∀ s2 ∈ P, s1 ≠ s2 → Disjoint s1 s2) ∧
   142→  (Finset.univ ⊆ P.biUnion id) ∧
   143→  (∀ s ∈ P, ∃ (u v : α) (p : G.Walk u v), p.IsPath ∧ s = p.support.toFinset)
   144→
   145→/-- Minimum size of a path cover of `G`. -/
   146→noncomputable def pathCoverNumber (G : SimpleGraph α) : ℕ :=
   147→  sInf { k | ∃ P : Finset (Finset α), P.card = k ∧ IsPathCover G P }
   148→
   149→/-- The same quantity as a real number. -/
   150→noncomputable def p (G : SimpleGraph α) : ℝ := (pathCoverNumber G : ℝ)
   151→
   152→/-- The Wiener index of `G`, which is the sum of distances between all
   153→pairs of vertices. -/
   154→noncomputable def wienerIndex (G : SimpleGraph α) : ℕ :=
   155→  ∑ uv : Sym2 α, uv.lift ⟨fun u v ↦ G.dist u v, by simp [dist_comm]⟩
   156→
   157→/-- Auxiliary function for Szeged index: counts vertices closer to u than v. -/
   158→noncomputable def szeged_aux (G : SimpleGraph α) (u v : α) : ℕ :=
   159→  (Finset.univ.filter (fun w => G.edist w u < G.edist w v)).card
   160→
   161→/-- The Szeged index of `G`.
   162→
   163→This is define as the sum `∑_{uv ∈ E(G)} n_u(u,v) * n_v(u,v)` where
   164→`n_u(uv)` is the number of vertices closer to `u` than `v`.
   165→-/
   166→noncomputable def szegedIndex (G : SimpleGraph α) [DecidableRel G.Adj] : ℕ :=
   167→  ∑ e ∈ G.edgeFinset,
   168→    e.lift ⟨fun u v => szeged_aux G u v * szeged_aux G v u, by simp [mul_comm]⟩
   169→
   170→/-- The average degree of `G`. -/
   171→noncomputable def averageDegree (G : SimpleGraph α) [DecidableRel G.Adj] : ℚ  :=
   172→  (∑ v, (G.degree v : ℚ)) / (Fintype.card α : ℚ)
   173→
   174→/-- The multiset of degrees of a graph. -/
   175→def degreeMultiset (G : SimpleGraph α) [DecidableRel G.Adj] : Multiset ℕ :=
   176→  Finset.univ.val.map fun v => G.degree v
   177→
   178→/-- The annihilation number of a graph. This is the largest number of degrees that can be added
   179→together without going over the total number of edges of that graph. -/
   180→def annihilationNumber (G : SimpleGraph α) [DecidableRel G.Adj] : ℕ :=
   181→  -- Calculate the limit: The number of edges (Sum of degrees / 2)
   182→  letI limit := G.edgeFinset.card
   183→
   184→  -- The set of all multisets of degrees that sum to less than or equal to `limit`
   185→  Finset.Iic (degreeMultiset G)
   186→    |>.filter (fun S ↦ Multiset.sum S ≤ limit)
   187→    |>.sup Multiset.card
   188→
   189→/--
   190→Computes the annihilation number of a graph G.
   191→
   192→It calculates the degree sequence, sorts it ascendingly, and finds the largest prefix length 'k'
   193→(where `0 ≤ k ≤ |V(G)|`) such that the sum of the prefix is less than or equal to the sum of the corresponding suffix.
   194→-/
   195→noncomputable def annihilationNumber' (G : SimpleGraph α) [DecidableRel G.Adj] : ℕ :=
   196→  -- 1. Get the degree sequence sorted in ascending order.
   197→  -- G.degree_list returns the list of degrees.
   198→  letI degrees := (Finset.univ.image fun v => G.degree v).sort (· ≤ ·)
   199→
   200→  -- 2. Define the condition for the annihilation number.
   201→  -- k represents the number of smallest degrees considered (the length of the prefix).
   202→  letI condition (k : ℕ) : Bool := (degrees.take k).sum ≤ (degrees.drop k).sum
   203→
   204→  -- 3. Find the maximum k in {0, ..., n} satisfying the condition.
   205→  -- List.range (n + 1) generates the list [0, 1, ..., n].
   206→  letI candidates := (List.range (Fintype.card α + 1)).filter condition
   207→
   208→  -- 4. Return the maximum candidate.
   209→  -- The list of candidates is guaranteed to be non-empty because k=0 always satisfies
   210→  -- the condition (0 ≤ sum of all degrees).
   211→  candidates.getLast!
   212→
   213→set_option linter.unusedSectionVars false in
   214→-- TODO(Paul-Lez): debug the issue with the unused variable linter...
   215→proof_wanted annihilationNumberEq (G : SimpleGraph α) [DecidableRel G.Adj] :
   216→    annihilationNumber G = annihilationNumber' G
   217→
   218→/-!
   219→## Residue
   220→The residue of a graph is the number of zeros remaining after iteratively applying the Havel-Hakimi
   221→algorithm to the degree sequence until all remaining degrees are zero.
   222→-/
   223→
   224→/--
   225→Helper function: Performs one step of the Havel-Hakimi reduction on a degree sequence.
   226→Assumes the input list `s` is sorted descending.
   227→Removes the first element `d`, decrements the next `d` elements by 1, and re-sorts the list descending.
   228→
   229→Note: when `s` is the list of vertices arising from a simple graph, if the first index is `s` then
   230→the degree list always has length at least `s+1` so this makes sense.
   231→-/
   232→private def havelHakimiStep (s : List ℕ) : List ℕ :=
   233→  match s with
   234→  | [] => []
   235→  | d :: rest =>
   236→    -- Split the rest into the part to decrement (first d elements) and the remaining part.
   237→    let (to_decrement, remaining) := rest.splitAt d
   238→    -- Decrement the elements
   239→    let decremented := to_decrement.map (· - 1)
   240→    -- Combine and re-sort descending.
   241→    (decremented ++ remaining).mergeSort (· ≥ ·)
   242→
   243→/--
   244→Auxiliary function to calculate the residue recursively.
   245→Applies Havel-Hakimi steps until the sequence consists only of zeros or is empty.
   246→-/
   247→private partial def residueAux : List ℕ → ℕ
   248→  | [] => 0        -- Empty sequence, residue is 0.
   249→  | 0 :: s => 1 + s.length -- If the largest degree is 0 (and the list is sorted), all are 0.
   250→  | s => residueAux (havelHakimiStep s) -- Apply one reduction step and recurse.
   251→
   252→/--
   253→Computes the residue of a graph G, ,i.e. the number of zeros remaining after iteratively applying the Havel-Hakimi
   254→algorithm to the degree sequence until all remaining degrees are zero.
   255→Starts with the descending degree sequence and applies the Havel-Hakimi process.
   256→-/
   257→noncomputable def residue (G : SimpleGraph α) [DecidableRel G.Adj] : ℕ :=
   258→  -- Get the degree sequence sorted in descending order and apply `residueAux`.
   259→  residueAux ((Finset.univ.image fun v => G.degree v).sort (· ≥ ·))
   260→
   261→/--
   262→Fractional alpha. This is defined as
   263→$$
   264→\max_x \sum_{v \in V} x_v
   265→$$
   266→subject to $0 \le x_v \le 1$ for all $v \in V$ and
   267→$x_u + x_v \le 1$ whenever $(u, v)$ are connected by an edge.
   268→-/
   269→noncomputable def fractionalAlpha (G : SimpleGraph α) [DecidableRel G.Adj] : ℝ :=
   270→  sSup {(∑ i, x i) | (x : α → ℝ) (_ : ∀ v, x v ∈ Set.Icc 0 1)
   271→    (_ : ∀ u v, G.Adj u v → x u + x v ≤ 1)}
   272→
   273→/--
   274→Lovász Theta Function (ϑ(G))
   275→The Lovász theta function is defined as:
   276→ϑ(G) = min λ_max(A)
   277→where the minimum is taken over all symmetric matrices A such that:
   278→
   279→A_ij = 1 for all i = j (diagonal entries are 1)
   280→A_ij = 0 for all {i,j} ∈ E (entries corresponding to edges are 0)
   281→A is positive semidefinite
   282→
   283→Here λ_max(A) denotes the maximum eigenvalue of A.
   284→-/
   285→noncomputable def lovaczThetaFunction
   286→    (G : SimpleGraph α) [DecidableRel G.Adj] : ℝ :=
   287→  sInf {(Matrix.IsHermitian.maxEigenvalue hA) | (A : Matrix α α ℝ) (hA : A.IsHermitian)
   288→      (_ : ∀ i, A i i = 1) (_ : ∀ i j, G.Adj i j → A i j = 0)}
   289→
   290→/--
   291→Given a simple graph `G` with adjacency matrix `A`, this is the number `n₀ + min n₊ n₋` where:
   292→- `n₀` is the multiplicity of zero as an eigenvalue of `A`
   293→- `n₊` is the number of positive eigenvalues of `A` (counting multiplicities)
   294→- `n₋` is the number of negative eigenvalues of `A` (counting multiplicities)
   295→-/
   296→noncomputable def cvetkovic (G : SimpleGraph α) [DecidableRel G.Adj] : ℕ :=
   297→  letI A : Matrix α α ℝ := G.adjMatrix ℝ
   298→  letI spectrum : Multiset ℝ := (Matrix.charpoly A).roots
   299→  letI positive_count : ℕ := spectrum.countP (fun x => 0 < x)
   300→  letI negative_count : ℕ := spectrum.countP (fun x => 0 > x)
   301→  letI zero_count : ℕ := spectrum.countP (fun x => 0 = x)
   302→  zero_count + min positive_count negative_count
   303→
   304→end SimpleGraph
   305→

    19→/-!
    20→# Erdős Problem 612
    21→
    22→*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/612](https://www.erdosproblems.com/612)
    23→
    24→Let $G$ be a connected graph with $n$ vertices, minimum degree $d$, and diameter $D$.
    25→
    26→The original conjecture of Erdős, Pach, Pollack, and Tuza [EPPT89] stated:
    27→- If $G$ contains no $K_{2r}$ and $(r-1)(3r+2) \mid d$ then
    28→  $D \leq \frac{2(r-1)(3r+2)}{2r^2-1} \cdot \frac{n}{d} + O(1)$
    29→- If $G$ contains no $K_{2r+1}$ and $(3r-1) \mid d$ then
    30→  $D \leq \frac{3r-1}{r} \cdot \frac{n}{d} + O(1)$
    31→
    32→The first case was disproven for $r \geq 2$ by Czabarka, Singgih, and Székely [CSS21].
    33→
    34→The amended conjecture (due to [CSS21]) states that if $G$ contains no $K_{k+1}$ then
    35→$D \leq (3 - 2/k) \cdot n/d + O(1)$.
    36→
    37→This is known:
    38→- For $k = 2$ ($K_3$-free), proved in [EPPT89]
    39→- For $3$-colourable graphs (weaker than $K_4$-free), by Czabarka, Dankelmann,
    40→  Székely [CDS09]
    41→- For $4$-colourable graphs (weaker than $K_5$-free), by Czabarka, Smith,
    42→  Székely [CSS23]
    43→
    44→It is also known that any connected graph on $n$ vertices with minimum degree $d$ has
    45→$D \leq 3n/(d+1) + O(1)$.
    46→
    47→[EPPT89] Erdős, P., Pach, J., Pollack, R., and Tuza, Zs., _Radius, diameter, and
    48→minimum degree_. J. Combin. Theory Ser. B 47 (1989), 73-79.
    49→
    50→[CSS21] Czabarka, É., Singgih, O., and Székely, L.A., _Counterexample to a conjecture
    51→of Erdős, Pach, Pollack, and Tuza on diameter and minimum degree_. 2021.
    52→
    53→[CDS09] Czabarka, É., Dankelmann, P., and Székely, L.A., _Diameter and degree
    54→conditions for $K_{t,t}$-free graphs_. 2009.
    55→
    56→[CSS23] Czabarka, É., Smith, S.J., and Székely, L.A., _Diameter bounds for
    57→4-colourable graphs_. 2023.
    58→-/
    59→

    61→

    64→/--
    65→**Erdős Problem 612** (Amended conjecture, [CSS21]):
    66→
    67→If $G$ is a connected $K_{k+1}$-free graph on $n$ vertices with minimum degree $d \geq 1$,
    68→then
    69→$$\operatorname{diam}(G) \leq \left(3 - \frac{2}{k}\right) \cdot \frac{n}{d} + C$$
    70→for some constant $C$ depending only on $k$.
    71→-/
    72→@[category research open, AMS 5]
    73→theorem erdos_612 (k : ℕ) (hk : k ≥ 2) :
    74→    ∃ C : ℝ, ∀ (n : ℕ) (G : SimpleGraph (Fin n)) [DecidableRel G.Adj],
    75→      G.Connected →
    76→      G.CliqueFree (k + 1) →
    77→      G.minDegree ≥ 1 →
    78→      (G.diam : ℝ) ≤ (3 - 2 / (k : ℝ)) * ((n : ℝ) / (G.minDegree : ℝ)) + C := by
    79→  sorry
    80→
    81→/--
    82→**Erdős Problem 612** (Known case $k = 2$, [EPPT89]):
    83→
    84→If $G$ is a connected triangle-free graph on $n$ vertices with minimum degree $d \geq 1$,
    85→then
    86→$$\operatorname{diam}(G) \leq 2 \cdot \frac{n}{d} + C$$
    87→for some absolute constant $C$.
    88→-/
    89→@[category research solved, AMS 5]
    90→theorem erdos_612.variants.triangle_free :
    91→    ∃ C : ℝ, ∀ (n : ℕ) (G : SimpleGraph (Fin n)) [DecidableRel G.Adj],
    92→      G.Connected →
    93→      G.CliqueFree 3 →
    94→      G.minDegree ≥ 1 →
    95→      (G.diam : ℝ) ≤ 2 * ((n : ℝ) / (G.minDegree : ℝ)) + C := by

    97→
    98→/--

    17→import FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports
    18→
    19→/-!
    20→# Erdős Problem 113
    21→
    22→*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/113](https://www.erdosproblems.com/113)
    23→
    24→Erdős–Simonovits conjecture (disproved by Janzer): is it true that for a bipartite graph $G$,
    25→the Turán number satisfies $\mathrm{ex}(n; G) \ll n^{3/2}$ if and only if $G$ is 2-degenerate?
    26→
    27→[ErSi84] Erdős, P. and Simonovits, M., *Cube-supersaturated graphs and related problems*,
    28→Progress in graph theory (Waterloo, Ont., 1982), Academic Press, Toronto, ON, 1984, 203–218.
    29→
    30→[Ja23b] Janzer, O., *Resolution of the Erdős–Simonovits conjecture on walks*, 2023.
    31→-/
    32→
    33→open SimpleGraph
    34→
    35→namespace Erdos113
    36→
    37→/-- An injective graph homomorphism from $H$ to $F$; witnesses that $F$ contains a
    38→subgraph isomorphic to $H$. -/
    39→def ContainsSubgraph {V U : Type*} (F : SimpleGraph V) (H : SimpleGraph U) : Prop :=
    40→  ∃ f : U → V, Function.Injective f ∧ ∀ u v : U, H.Adj u v → F.Adj (f u) (f v)
    41→
    42→/-- The Turán number $\operatorname{ex}(n; H)$: the maximum number of edges in a simple graph
    43→on $n$ vertices that contains no copy of $H$ as a subgraph. -/
    44→noncomputable def turanNumber {U : Type*} (H : SimpleGraph U) (n : ℕ) : ℕ :=
    45→  sSup {m : ℕ | ∃ (V : Type) (fv : Fintype V) (F : SimpleGraph V) (dr : DecidableRel F.Adj),
    46→    haveI := fv; haveI := dr;
    47→    Fintype.card V = n ∧ ¬ContainsSubgraph F H ∧ F.edgeFinset.card = m}
    48→
    49→/-- A graph $G$ is 2-degenerate if every non-empty finite set of vertices contains
    50→a vertex with at most 2 neighbors within that set. Equivalently, $G$ has no
    51→induced subgraph with minimum degree at least 3. -/
    52→def IsTwoDegenerateGraph {V : Type*} (G : SimpleGraph V) : Prop :=
    53→  ∀ (S : Set V), S.Finite → S.Nonempty →
    54→    ∃ v ∈ S, (G.neighborSet v ∩ S).ncard ≤ 2
    55→
    56→/--
    57→Erdős–Simonovits Conjecture (Problem #113) [ErSi84]:
    58→For a bipartite graph $G$ on a finite vertex set, the Turán number satisfies
    59→$$
    60→  \operatorname{ex}(n; G) \ll n^{3/2}
    61→$$
    62→if and only if $G$ is 2-degenerate (i.e., $G$ has no induced subgraph with minimum
    63→degree at least 3).
    64→
    65→Here $\operatorname{ex}(n; G) \ll n^{3/2}$ means there exists a constant $C > 0$ such that
    66→$\operatorname{ex}(n; G) \leq C \cdot n^{3/2}$ for all $n$.
    67→
    68→DISPROVED by Janzer [Ja23b]: for any $\varepsilon > 0$, there exists a 3-regular bipartite
    69→graph $H$ such that $\operatorname{ex}(n; H) \ll n^{4/3 + \varepsilon}$. Since a 3-regular graph is not
    70→2-degenerate but its Turán number is still $o(n^{3/2})$, the "only if" direction
    71→($\operatorname{ex}(n; G) \ll n^{3/2} \to G$ is 2-degenerate) fails.
    72→-/
    73→@[category research solved, AMS 5]
    74→theorem erdos_113 : answer(False) ↔
    75→    ∀ (U : Type*) (G : SimpleGraph U) [Fintype U] [DecidableRel G.Adj],
    76→      Nonempty (G.Coloring (Fin 2)) →
    77→      (IsTwoDegenerateGraph G ↔
    78→        ∃ C : ℝ, 0 < C ∧ ∀ n : ℕ,
    79→          (turanNumber G n : ℝ) ≤ C * (n : ℝ) ^ (3 / 2 : ℝ)) := by
    80→  sorry
    81→
    82→end Erdos113
    83→
